# Welcome to MetaDoc

This is an AI-powered word processing software that helps you quickly generate content.

setting.lineNumber: Display Line Numbers in Code Blocks
setting.excludeCodeBlocks: Exclude Code Blocks When Counting Text
setting.excludeCodeHint: Code may affect word frequency statistics. You can choose to exclude it.
setting.autoDownloadHint: If enabled, external images will be saved locally on import. Otherwise, they won't display offline. Requires restart.
setting.parseEmbeddedImagesHint: When disabled, images inside uploaded docx/pptx/pdf files are not OCRed; direct image uploads are still OCRed.

image.title: Image Settings
image.titleHint: Image upload settings, takes effect after reopening the file
image.insertActionSaveToDocumentDir: Save in the same directory as the document
image.insertActionHint: Select the default action when inserting images.
image.keepNetworkImageUrl: Keep network image URL (Disable auto download)
image.keepNetworkImageUrlHint: When checked, network images will keep their original URL and will not be downloaded locally. If unchecked, network images will be automatically downloaded and saved as local images, so you can view the document locally without internet connection.
image.syntaxPreferencesHint: Configure syntax format preferences for image paths.
image.addDotSlashHint: Add ./ prefix to relative paths for better readability.
image.autoEscapeImageUrl: Automatically escape URL when inserting network images
image.autoEscapeImageUrlHint: For example, when inserting 'img/示例图片.png', the image path will automatically change to 'img/%E7%A4%BA%E4...png'. Enabling this option can achieve better compatibility with other Markdown engines, while disabling this option can achieve higher readability.
image.localImageDirPlaceholder: Leave empty to use default path (Pictures/meta-doc-imgs)
image.localImageDirHint: Set the local image storage directory. Leave empty to use the default path.
image.copyToDocumentDirHint: When enabled, images will be saved to the document directory first. If it's a new document (unsaved), it will fall back to the upload directory.
image.customUploadApiFieldNameHint: The form field name used by the upload API to receive files.

setting.defaultEditorModeHint: Editor mode used when opening documents. You can switch it from the editor toolbar anytime.
setting.editorModeWysiwygHint: Friendly for users unfamiliar with Markdown; works seamlessly for Markdown users too.
setting.editorModeIrHint: Familiar to Typora users; one of the most elegant ways to edit Markdown.
setting.editorModeSvHint: Traditional split preview mode, suitable for large screens.
setting.metadocHint: MetaDoc’s official LLM, supporting various models.
setting.ollamaHint: Locally hosted LLM platform supporting multiple models.
setting.openaiHint: Any LLM compatible with the OpenAI API standard.
setting.llmTemperatureHint: Controls the randomness of AI output. Higher values are more random. Recommended: 1.3
setting.testPrompt: Hello! Please introduce yourself in a short way.
setting.manualHint: Manually input simulated API responses, only available in development environment
setting.confirmDeleteConfig: Are you sure you want to delete this config?
setting.manualConfigHint: Manual mode: Manually input API responses through Express server's simulated interface
setting.manualTokenInputPlaceholder: Enter manual LLM response content here (for testing and debugging)
setting.particleEffectHint: Enable particle effects to enhance visual aesthetics. This may impact performance.
setting.knowledgeBaseTooltip: When enabled, the content of AI will be supported by information from the local knowledge base.
setting.autoCompletionFullModeHint: AI will output after full content generation (recommended)
setting.autoCompletionStreamModeHint: AI will produce content in real time.
setting.knowledgeBaseScoreThresholdTooltip: Only query results with a similarity above that value will be accepted by AI, recommendation 0.5.
setting.loggingFilterPlaceholder: Enter module name to filter, e.g.: ai-graph
setting.loggingFilterHint: Filter logs by module name, e.g. enter "ai-graph" to show only logs from that module and its sub-modules
setting.logRetentionPeriodHint: Automatically clean up old log files that exceed the retention period. Cleanup runs automatically when the application starts
setting.deepseekHint: The major AI model provided by the official DeepSeek supports multiple models
setting.geminiHint: The major AI model provided by Google Gemini supports multiple models
setting.openaiOfficialHint: API from OpenAI, which supports models

latexEditor.pdfEmpty: Please compile the LaTeX code correctly. The PDF will be shown here.

commandDocs.usepackage: Load a LaTeX package. Example: \usepackage{graphicx} or \usepackage[utf8]{inputenc}
commandDocs.include: Include another LaTeX file with page break. Example: \include{appendix}
commandDocs.tiny: Tiny font size. Example: {\tiny smallest text}
commandDocs.scriptsize: Script size font. Example: {\scriptsize small text}
commandDocs.footnotesize: Footnote size font. Example: {\footnotesize footnote text}
commandDocs.small: Small font size. Example: {\small small text}
commandDocs.normalsize: Normal font size. Example: {\normalsize normal text}
commandDocs.large: Large font size. Example: {\large large text}
commandDocs.Large: Larger font size. Example: {\Large larger text}
commandDocs.LARGE: Very large font size. Example: {\LARGE very large}
commandDocs.huge: Huge font size. Example: {\huge huge text}
commandDocs.Huge: Largest font size. Example: {\Huge largest text}
commandDocs.centering: Center text. Example: \centering (declaration, affects following text)
commandDocs.newline: Line break. Example: text\newline next line
commandDocs.linebreak: Line break with penalty. Example: text\linebreak
commandDocs.clearpage: Clear page and start new page. Example: \clearpage
commandDocs.itemize: Unordered list environment. Example: \begin{itemize} \item First \item Second \end{itemize}
commandDocs.enumerate: Ordered list environment. Example: \begin{enumerate} \item First \item Second \end{enumerate}
commandDocs.item: List item. Example: \item First item or \item[Label] Item with label
commandDocs.caption: Caption for float. Example: \caption{Figure description}
commandDocs.label: Create label for cross-reference. Example: \label{fig:myfigure}
commandDocs.alignat: Aligned equations with spacing control. Example: \begin{alignat}{2} ... \end{alignat}
commandDocs.sqrt: Square root. Example: \sqrt{x} or \sqrt[n]{x} for nth root
commandDocs.iint: Double integral. Example: \iint_D f(x,y) dx dy
commandDocs.iiint: Triple integral. Example: \iiint_V f(x,y,z) dx dy dz
commandDocs.leq: Less than or equal (≤). Example: $x \leq y$
commandDocs.sqsubseteq: Square subset or equal (⊑). Example: $A \sqsubseteq B$
commandDocs.geq: Greater than or equal (≥). Example: $x \geq y$
commandDocs.sqsupseteq: Square superset or equal (⊒). Example: $A \sqsupseteq B$
commandDocs.Longleftarrow: Long double left arrow (⟸). Example: $x \Longleftarrow y$
commandDocs.Longrightarrow: Long double right arrow (⟹). Example: $x \Longrightarrow y$
commandDocs.Longleftrightarrow: Long double left-right arrow (⟺). Example: $x \Longleftrightarrow y$
commandDocs.quad: Quad space (1em). Example: text\quad more text
commandDocs.qquad: Double quad space (2em). Example: text\qquad more text
commandDocs.title: Document title. Example: \title{My Document}
commandDocs.author: Document author. Example: \author{John Doe}
commandDocs.url: URL (requires url or hyperref package). Example: \url{https://example.com}

overview.title: Overview
overview.description: This page helps you understand your usage profile and recommends a learning path. You can follow the path or jump to any chapter from the left sidebar.
overview.profileSummarySentence: You're mainly in the "{scenario}" scenario; currently Markdown: {markdown}, LaTeX: {latex}, AI Agent: {agent}.
overview.noProfile: Haven't set your profile yet? Tell us your preferences so we can recommend a learning path.

profile.title: User Profile Questionnaire
profile.scenarioHint: Please select your main usage scenario, which will help us recommend the most suitable learning path for you
profile.reanalyzeConfirmMessage: Resetting your profile will clear your current learning progress. Continue?

hint.student: Student: Mainly for course notes, homework documents, study materials organization, etc.
hint.researcher: Researcher: Mainly for academic papers, research reports, experiment records, etc.
hint.it: IT Professional: Mainly for technical documentation, code comments, API documentation, project documentation, etc.
hint.office: Office Worker: Mainly for meeting minutes, work reports, business documents, etc.

tooltip.student: Student: Mainly for course notes, homework documents, study materials organization, etc.
tooltip.researcher: Researcher: Mainly for academic papers, research reports, experiment records, etc.
tooltip.it: IT Professional: Mainly for technical documentation, code comments, API documentation, project documentation, etc.
tooltip.office: Office Worker: Mainly for meeting minutes, work reports, business documents, etc.

markdownLevelHints.none: Completely unfamiliar with Markdown syntax, need to start learning from basic syntax
markdownLevelHints.basic: Know basic syntax (headings, lists, bold, etc.), can write simple documents
markdownLevelHints.intermediate: Proficient in Markdown syntax, familiar with advanced features like tables, code blocks, links, etc.
markdownLevelHints.advanced: Expert in Markdown, familiar with extended syntax, math formulas, charts, and other advanced features

latexLevelHints.none: Completely unfamiliar with LaTeX, need to start learning from basic syntax and document structure
latexLevelHints.basic: Know basic syntax and document structure, can write simple LaTeX documents
latexLevelHints.intermediate: Proficient in LaTeX, familiar with common features like math formulas, tables, image insertion, etc.
latexLevelHints.advanced: Expert in LaTeX, familiar with advanced features like package usage, custom commands, bibliography management, etc.

agentLevels.heard: Heard of it but don't understand

profile.usedLatexEditorHint: Such as Overleaf, TeXStudio, TeXworks, TeXShop and other LaTeX editors
profile.usedOtherMarkdownEditor: Have you used other Markdown editors?
profile.usedOtherMarkdownEditorHint: Such as VS Code, Obsidian, Notion, Yuque and other editors that support Markdown
profile.markdownEditorHint: Please tell us about your Markdown editor experience

fileOperations.title: File Operations
fileOperations.content:
# File Operations

MetaDoc supports various file operations including creating, opening, and saving documents.

## Create Document

Click "File" → "New" in the left menu, or use `Ctrl+N` to create a new document.

## Open Document

- Click "File" → "Open" in the left menu, or use `Ctrl+O`
- Supports `.md` (Markdown) and `.tex` (LaTeX) files
- Recently opened documents are shown in the "Recent Documents" list on the home page

## Save Document

- Use `Ctrl+S` to save the current document
- Use `Ctrl+Shift+S` to save as
- Use `Ctrl+K S` to save all open documents

## Export Document

Supports exporting to multiple formats:

- **PDF**: Suitable for printing and sharing
- **HTML**: Suitable for web display
- **DOCX**: Suitable for Word editing
- **LaTeX**: Suitable for academic papers

Select the desired format from "File" → "Export" menu.

editor.title: Editor Usage
editor.content:
# Editor Usage

MetaDoc provides two editors: Markdown editor and LaTeX editor.

## Markdown Editor

- Based on Vditor with real-time preview
- Syntax highlighting and auto-completion
- Supports math formulas, code blocks, tables, etc.
- Shortcuts:
  - `Ctrl+B`: Bold
  - `Ctrl+I`: Italic
  - `Ctrl+K`: Insert link

## LaTeX Editor

- Based on Monaco Editor for professional LaTeX editing
- Real-time PDF preview compilation
- Syntax checking and error hints
- Supports automatic package downloads

## Multi-tab Operations

- Can open multiple documents simultaneously
- Use `Ctrl+Tab` to quickly switch tabs
- Drag tabs to reorder
- Right-click tabs for pin, close, move to new window, etc.

multiTab.title: Multi-tab Operations
multiTab.content:
# Multi-tab Operations

MetaDoc supports browser-like multi-tab management.

## Basic Operations

- **New Tab**: Click the `+` button on the right side of the tab bar
- **Switch Tabs**: Click tabs or use `Ctrl+Tab`
- **Close Tab**: Click `×` on tabs or use `Ctrl+W`
- **Restore Closed Tab**: Use `Ctrl+Shift+T`

## Tab Management

```mermaid
graph LR
A[New Document] --> B[Open Document]
B --> C[Edit Document]
C --> D[Save Document]
D --> E[Close Tab]
```

## Pin Tabs

Right-click a tab and select "Pin Tab". Pinned tabs always appear on the left and cannot be closed.

## Tab Dragging

- Drag tabs to reorder
- Drag tabs out of window to create new window
- Drag tabs to other windows to merge

multiWindow.title: Multi-window Operations
multiWindow.content:
# Multi-window Operations

MetaDoc supports multi-window management, allowing you to open different documents in different windows.

## Create New Window

- Drag a tab out of the window boundary
- Right-click a tab and select "Open in New Window"
- Use `Ctrl+N` to create a document in a new window

## Switch Between Windows

- Use `Alt+Tab` to switch windows
- Each window has its own tab list

## Merge Windows

Drag tabs from one window to another to merge.

export.title: Export Function
export.content:
# Export Function

MetaDoc supports exporting documents to multiple formats.

## Supported Export Formats

| Source Format | Supported Export Formats |
|--------------|------------------------|
| Markdown | MD, HTML, DOCX, PDF, TEX |
| LaTeX | TEX, PDF, MD, HTML, DOCX |

## Export Steps

1. Open the document to export
2. Click "File" → "Export"
3. Select target format
4. Configure export options (such as PDF page size, HTML styles, etc.)
5. Click "Export" button

## Export Options

Different formats have different export options:

- **PDF**: Page size, margins, headers and footers, etc.
- **HTML**: CSS styles, whether to embed resources, etc.
- **DOCX**: Template selection, style mapping, etc.

basic.title: Markdown Basics
basic.content:
# Markdown Basics

Markdown is a lightweight markup language for writing formatted documents.

## Headings

Use `#` for headings, the number of `#` indicates the heading level:

```markdown
# Level 1 Heading
## Level 2 Heading
### Level 3 Heading
```

## Text Formatting

- **Bold**: `**text**` or `__text__`
- *Italic*: `*text*` or `_text_`
- ~~Strikethrough~~: `~~text~~`
- `Code`: Wrap with backticks

## Lists

### Unordered List

```markdown
- Item 1
- Item 2
  - Sub-item 2.1
  - Sub-item 2.2
```

### Ordered List

```markdown
1. First item
2. Second item
3. Third item
```

## Links and Images

```markdown
[Link text](https://example.com)
![Image description](image.png)
```

## Code Blocks

Wrap code with three backticks:

````markdown
```javascript
function hello() {
  console.log('Hello, World!');
}
```
````

## Tables

```markdown
| Col1 | Col2 | Col3 |
|------|------|------|
| Data1 | Data2 | Data3 |
| Data4 | Data5 | Data6 |
```

advanced.title: Markdown Advanced Features
advanced.content:
# Markdown Advanced Features ## Math Formulas ### Inline Formulas Use `$formula$` for inline formulas: For example: $E = mc^2$ ### Block Formulas Use `$$formula$$` for block formulas: $$\int_{-\infty}^{\infty} e^{-x^2} dx = \sqrt{\pi}$$ ## Charts MetaDoc supports various chart types, see the "Chart Drawing" section for details. ## Task Lists ```markdown - [x] Completed task - [ ] Incomplete task ``` ## Blockquotes ```markdown > This is a blockquote > Can be multiple lines ``` ## Horizontal Rules ```markdown --- ``` ## HTML Support Markdown supports embedded HTML: ```html
Red text
```

basic.title: LaTeX Basics
basic.content:
# LaTeX Basics

LaTeX is a professional document typesetting system, especially suitable for academic papers and technical documents.

## Document Structure

```latex
\documentclass{article}
\begin{document}
\section{Section Title}
Document content...
\end{document}
```

## Common Commands

- `\section{}`: Level 1 section
- `\subsection{}`: Level 2 section
- `\textbf{}`: Bold
- `\textit{}`: Italic
- `\emph{}`: Emphasis

## Math Formulas

### Inline Formulas

Use `$formula$` or `\(formula\)`:

For example: $a^2 + b^2 = c^2$

### Block Formulas

Use `$$formula$$` or `\[formula\]`:

$$\sum_{i=1}^{n} i = \frac{n(n+1)}{2}$$

## Lists

```latex
\begin{itemize}
  \item Item 1
  \item Item 2
\end{itemize}
```

## Tables

```latex
\begin{tabular}{|c|c|c|}
\hline
Col1 & Col2 & Col3 \\
\hline
Data1 & Data2 & Data3 \\
\hline
\end{tabular}
```

advanced.title: LaTeX Advanced
advanced.content:
# LaTeX Advanced

## Package Management

Use `\usepackage{}` to import packages:

```latex
\usepackage{amsmath}
\usepackage{graphicx}
```

MetaDoc automatically downloads missing packages.

## Bibliography

Use BibTeX to manage references:

```latex
\bibliographystyle{plain}
\bibliography{references}
```

## Inserting Figures

```latex
\begin{figure}[h]
  \centering
  \includegraphics{image.png}
  \caption{Figure Title}
\end{figure}
```

## Custom Commands

```latex
\newcommand{\mycommand}[1]{Format of #1}
```

## Compilation

MetaDoc uses Tectonic compiler, supporting:

- Real-time compilation preview
- Automatic package downloads
- Error location and hints

introduction.title: Chart Drawing Introduction
introduction.content:
# Chart Drawing Introduction

MetaDoc supports multiple chart engines to create various types of charts.

## Supported Chart Engines

| Engine | Use Cases |
|--------|----------|
| Mermaid | Flowcharts, UML diagrams, Gantt charts |
| PlantUML | Professional UML modeling |
| ECharts | Data visualization charts |
| Flowchart | Basic flowcharts |
| Mindmap | Mind maps |

## Inserting Charts

Use code blocks in Markdown documents to insert charts:

````markdown
```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Start] --> B[Process]
    B --> C[End]
```
````

## Chart Rendering

- Charts are rendered in real-time in the editor
- Supports export to PDF, HTML and other formats
- Chart code supports i18n multi-language

mermaid.title: Mermaid Charts
mermaid.content:
# Mermaid Charts

Mermaid is suitable for quickly drawing flowcharts, sequence diagrams, and project planning charts.

## Flowchart

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Start] --> B{Decision}
    B -->|Yes| C[Action1]
    B -->|No| D[Action2]
    C --> E[End]
    D --> E
```

## Sequence Diagram

```mermaid
sequenceDiagram
    participant A as User
    participant B as System
    A->>B: Request
    B-->>A: Response
```

## UML Class Diagram

```mermaid
classDiagram
    class Animal {
        +String name
        +int age
        +eat()
    }
    class Dog {
        +bark()
    }
    Animal <|-- Dog
```

## Gantt Chart

```mermaid
gantt
    title Project Plan
    dateFormat YYYY-MM-DD
    section Phase 1
    Task1 :a1, 2024-01-01, 30d
    Task2 :a2, after a1, 20d
```

## Notes

- It is recommended to use `["..."]` to wrap strings to avoid escape errors
- Avoid identifiers with spaces or special characters in class diagrams

llmConfig.title: LLM Configuration Guide
llmConfig.content:
# LLM Configuration Guide

MetaDoc supports multiple LLM APIs. You can configure your own API keys as needed.

## Supported LLM APIs

MetaDoc supports the following LLM services:

- **OpenAI**: GPT-3.5, GPT-4, etc.
- **DeepSeek**: DeepSeek Chat, etc.
- **Ollama**: Locally deployed models
- **Gemini**: Google Gemini
- **MetaDoc**: MetaDoc official API
- **Custom API**: Custom APIs compatible with OpenAI format

## Configuration Steps

1. Open "Settings" → "LLM Configuration"
2. Select LLM type
3. Enter API key (if required)
4. Select model
5. Configure other options (temperature, max tokens, etc.)
6. Click "Save"

## API Key Acquisition

### OpenAI

1. Visit https://platform.openai.com/api-keys
2. Log in
3. Create a new API key
4. Copy the key to MetaDoc

### DeepSeek

1. Visit https://platform.deepseek.com/api_keys
2. Log in
3. Create API key
4. Copy the key to MetaDoc

### Ollama (Local Deployment)

1. Install Ollama: https://ollama.ai
2. Run `ollama serve`
3. Configure API URL in MetaDoc as `http://localhost:11434/api`
4. Select installed model

## Notes

- Keep API keys secure, do not leak
- Using APIs incurs costs, please monitor usage
- Some APIs may require VPN to access

tools.title: AI Tools Usage
tools.content:
# AI Tools Usage

MetaDoc provides rich AI tools to help improve document creation efficiency.

## Available Tools

### 1. AI Chat

Chat with AI to get help and suggestions.

- Click "AI Assistant" → "Chat with AI" in the left menu
- Or use shortcut keys to open AI chat window
- Supports context understanding, can chat based on current document content

### 2. Formula Recognition

Recognize mathematical formulas in handwritten or image format.

- Click "AI Assistant" → "Handwriting Formula Recognition"
- Upload image or use camera to take photo
- AI automatically recognizes formulas and converts to LaTeX format

### 3. Smart Drawing Assistant

Use AI to generate chart code.

- Describe the chart you want
- AI generates corresponding Mermaid, PlantUML or ECharts code
- Can be directly inserted into document

### 4. Data Analysis Tool

Analyze data tables in documents.

- Select table in document
- Click "AI Assistant" → "Data Analysis Tool"
- AI analyzes data and generates visualization charts

### 5. OCR Text Recognition

Recognize text in images.

- Click "AI Assistant" → "OCR Text Recognition"
- Upload image
- AI recognizes and extracts text from image

### 6. Attachment Parser Tool

Parse PDF, Word and other attachment files.

- Click "AI Assistant" → "Attachment Parser Tool"
- Upload attachment file
- AI extracts file content and adds to knowledge base

## Usage Tips

- Using RAG knowledge base can improve AI answer accuracy
- Referencing document content in chat can get more relevant answers
- Proper use of tools can greatly improve work efficiency

chat.title: AI Chat Usage
chat.content:
# AI Chat Usage

The AI chat feature allows you to have multi-turn conversations with AI and save conversation history.

## Create Chat

1. Open AI chat window
2. Click "New Chat"
3. Enter chat name (optional)
4. Start chatting

## Chat Management

- **Switch Chat**: Select different chats in chat list
- **Rename Chat**: Right-click chat and select "Rename"
- **Delete Chat**: Right-click chat and select "Delete"
- **Export Chat**: Right-click chat and select "Export", can export as Markdown format

## Chat Features

### Context Understanding

AI chat remembers previous conversation content and can answer based on context.

### Document Reference

Can reference currently open document content in chat:

- Select text in document
- Type "Please analyze this text" in AI chat
- AI analyzes based on selected text

### Knowledge Base Retrieval

After enabling RAG knowledge base, AI retrieves relevant information from knowledge base to answer questions.

## Shortcuts

- `Ctrl+L`: Open/close AI chat window
- `Enter`: Send message
- `Shift+Enter`: New line
- `Ctrl+/`: Clear input box

introduction.title: Agent Usage Tutorial
introduction.content:
# Agent Usage Tutorial

Agent is one of MetaDoc's core features. It is an intelligent assistant that can automatically execute complex tasks.

## What is Agent

Agent is an LLM-based intelligent system that can:

- Understand your intent
- Plan execution steps
- Call various tools to complete tasks
- Automatically correct errors

## How Agent Works

Agent uses a "think-act-observe" loop:

1. **Think**: Analyze task, make plan
2. **Act**: Call tools to perform operations
3. **Observe**: Check results, determine if need to continue
4. **Reflect**: Evaluate execution effect, adjust strategy

## Use Cases

Agent is suitable for:

- Document content generation and optimization
- Automatic chart generation and insertion
- Document structure optimization
- Batch processing tasks

## Getting Started

1. Open "Agent" view
2. Create new Agent session
3. Enter task description
4. Agent automatically executes task

## Notes

- Agent consumes API calls, please monitor costs
- Complex tasks may take longer
- Recommend starting with small tasks to familiarize with Agent usage

capabilities.title: Agent Capabilities
capabilities.content:
# Agent Capabilities

Agent supports various capabilities to help you complete various document processing tasks.

## Core Capabilities

### 1. Document Editing

- Insert, delete, replace text
- Format text
- Adjust document structure

### 2. Outline Operations

- Generate document outline
- Optimize outline structure
- Add subsections
- Generate section content

### 3. Chart Generation

- Automatically generate charts based on content
- Insert Mermaid, PlantUML, ECharts code
- Optimize chart layout

### 4. Content Generation

- Generate content based on outline
- Expand documents
- Polish text

### 5. Knowledge Retrieval

- Retrieve relevant information from knowledge base
- Generate content based on retrieval results

## Tool Collection

Agent can use the following tools:

- `edit`: Edit document content
- `outline-tree`: Get document outline
- `outline-optimize`: Optimize outline structure
- `chart-generation`: Generate charts
- `rag-query`: Query knowledge base
- etc...

## Limitations

- Agent execution is limited by available tools
- Some operations may require user confirmation
- Complex tasks may require multiple interactions

risks.title: Agent Risks and Costs
risks.content:
# Agent Risks and Costs

When using Agent, please note the following risks and costs.

## Risks

### 1. Content Accuracy

- Agent-generated content may not be completely accurate
- Recommend manual review and modification
- Use with caution for important documents

### 2. Unexpected Modifications

- Agent may modify content you don't want modified
- Recommend saving documents before use
- Can use version control features

### 3. Privacy and Security

- Agent sends document content to LLM API
- Use with caution for sensitive information
- Recommend using locally deployed LLM (such as Ollama)

## Costs

### API Call Fees

Using Agent incurs API call fees:

- **OpenAI GPT-4**: About $0.03-0.06 per 1000 tokens
- **OpenAI GPT-3.5**: About $0.001-0.002 per 1000 tokens
- **DeepSeek**: About $0.0001-0.0002 per 1000 tokens
- **Ollama**: Free (local deployment)

### Token Consumption

Agent token consumption depends on:

- Document length
- Task complexity
- Number of tool calls

A medium complexity task may consume:

- Input: 1000-5000 tokens
- Output: 500-2000 tokens
- Tool calls: 500-2000 tokens

Total: 2000-9000 tokens

## Optimization Suggestions

1. **Use Local LLM**: If possible, use locally deployed LLM like Ollama
2. **Reasonable Settings**: Adjust temperature, max tokens and other parameters
3. **Batch Processing**: Split large tasks into small tasks
4. **Monitor Usage**: Regularly check LLM statistics to understand usage

## Cost Estimation

Assuming using Agent to process 10 documents per day, each document averages 5000 tokens:

- Using GPT-3.5: About $0.05-0.10/day
- Using GPT-4: About $1.50-3.00/day
- Using DeepSeek: About $0.005-0.01/day
- Using Ollama: $0/day

philosophy.title: MetaDoc Design Philosophy
philosophy.content:
# MetaDoc Design Philosophy

MetaDoc's design follows the following core principles.

## Bootstrapping Design

The user manual itself is written in Markdown, demonstrating MetaDoc's capabilities. This reflects the "bootstrapping" design philosophy: using the product itself to showcase the product.

## Document-Driven

MetaDoc is a "document-driven" system:

- Documents are the core, all features revolve around documents
- Supports multiple document formats (Markdown, LaTeX)
- Documents can be exported to multiple formats

## Intelligence

Through AI and Agent technology, make document creation more intelligent:

- AI-assisted writing and proofreading
- Agent automatically executes complex tasks
- Intelligent chart generation

## Extensibility

- Supports custom tools
- Plugin architecture
- Open API interface

## User Experience

- Clean and beautiful interface
- Smooth operation experience
- Rich shortcut key support
- Multi-language support

## Technology Stack

- **Frontend**: Vue 3 + TypeScript
- **Desktop**: Electron
- **Editor**: Monaco + Vditor
- **AI**: LLM Agent framework
- **Database**: SQLite + sqlite-vec

## Future Plans

- More AI capabilities
- Better collaboration features
- Cloud sync
- Mobile support
